Description: Use the official Tailwind integration for automatic setup: PostCSS config, class scanning, and v4 support
Do: Run npx astro add tailwind for zero-config setup
Don't: Manually configure PostCSS and Tailwind when the official integration exists

Description: Use Astro built-in style scoping by writing style blocks directly in .astro components; no integration needed
Do: Write style tags in each component for scoped styles without any CSS framework
Don't: Import a global CSS framework just to style one component

Description: Define semantic design tokens as CSS custom properties in :root for consistent colors, spacing, and typography across all components
Do: Use :root variables in a global CSS file or BaseLayout is:global style block
Don't: Hardcode color hex values and spacing numbers in every component

Description: Use the MDX integration to write pages and components that mix Markdown and Astro components
Do: Add @astrojs/mdx for blog posts or docs that need embedded components
Don't: Use plain .md files when you need interactive components inside your content
Code Bad: Plain markdown with HTML tables instead of components

Description: Use the Content Layer API to define collections with custom loaders that pull from local files, CMS, databases, or APIs
Do: Define collections in src/content.config.ts with a loader and Zod schema
Don't: Hard-code content as const arrays inside .astro component frontmatter

Description: Use getCollection() with filtering and sorting to query content at build time. Results are fully typed based on your Zod schemas
Do: Call getCollection with filter function for draft filtering and sort by date
Don't: Use import.meta.glob() for structured content that should use content collections

Description: Generates a sitemap.xml at build time automatically from all static routes
Do: npx astro add sitemap and configure customPages for dynamic routes
Don't: Forget to add a sitemap or write one manually

Description: Generate RSS, Atom, or JSON feeds for blog or content sites using the rss() helper
Do: Create a pages/rss.xml.ts endpoint that calls rss() with your content collection
Don't: Have a blog without an RSS feed

Description: Offload third-party scripts like Google Analytics to a web worker so they do not block the main thread
Do: npx astro add partytown and add type=text/partytown to third-party scripts
Don't: Load analytics and tracking scripts synchronously in head
Code Good:
npx astro add partytown
<script type=text/partytown>
  // Google Analytics tracking code
</script>

Description: Use astro-compress to minify HTML, CSS, JS and compress images during build
Do: Add astro-compress to integrations in astro.config
Don't: Deploy unminified HTML with comments and whitespace

Description: Add React support for interactive components inside Astro pages. Use client directives for hydration control
Do: Use @astrojs/react and add client:load only to components that need interactivity
Don't: Migrate your entire app to React components or hydrate static components

Description: Astro supports multiple frameworks simultaneously. Choose the right one for each interactive island
Do: Use @astrojs/vue, @astrojs/svelte, or @astrojs/solid-js depending on your team and needs
Don't: Install every framework just because you can
Code Good:
npx astro add vue
npx astro add svelte
npx astro add solid-js
Code Bad: All 6 renderers installed when only one is needed

Description: Deploy to Cloudflare Pages with SSR support. Astro 6 has native Cloudflare adapter integration via @astrojs/cloudflare
Do: Use @astrojs/cloudflare adapter and configure output: server or hybrid
Don't: Deploy SSR site to Cloudflare without the adapter

Description: Deploy to Vercel with SSR or static output via @astrojs/vercel
Do: npx astro add vercel and configure with appropriate output mode
Don't: Commit .vercel directory or use vercel.json when adapter handles it
Code Good:
npx astro add vercel
adapter: vercel()

Description: Build a standalone Node.js server with @astrojs/node for containerized deployment or traditional VPS hosting
Do: Use @astrojs/node with output: server and mode: standalone
Don't: Use the Node adapter when deploying to serverless platforms that have their own adapter
Code Good:
npx astro add node
adapter: node({ mode: 'standalone' })

Guideline: Privacy-first analytics with Plausible or Umami
Description: Add lightweight analytics without compromising performance: use a script tag with defer or Partytown
Do: Use a simple script defer tag in your BaseLayout; no integration needed
Don't: Load heavy analytics SDKs that block rendering

Description: Configure fonts from local files or providers (Google, Fontsource). Astro auto-downloads, caches self-hosted copies, generates fallbacks, and adds preload hints
Do: Use the Font component from astro:fonts to apply fonts anywhere
Don't: Load fonts from Google Fonts CDN directly without self-hosting when the Fonts API exists
Code Good:
import { Font } from 'astro:fonts';
<Font provider=google family=Inter weights={[400, 600, 700]} />

Description: Add authentication with Auth.js for Astro. Supports OAuth, magic links, and credentials
Do: Use @auth/astro for server-side auth with session management
Don't: Build custom auth from scratch when a battle-tested library exists
Code Good:
npm install @auth/astro
// Configure providers in auth.config.ts
